title: FDA Clears First Cellular Rejuvenation Trial, Anchoring $120B Longevity Market
description: Equity-Insider coverage spotlights the FDA's first cleared cellular rejuvenation trial and a longevity market on pace from $85B in 2025 to ~$120B by 2030, with $8.49B in private capital deployed across 325 deals last year. Combined with GLP-1 formally classified as 'longevity therapeutics,' the regulatory signal marks a watershed for aging drug development.

title: Rockefeller 7-Million-Cell Atlas Shows Aging Is a Coordinated Whole-Body Program
description: A Science paper from Junyue Cao's Rockefeller lab maps nearly 7 million cells across 21 mouse tissues, showing that aging begins earlier than expected and is synchronized across organs via shared blood-borne signals and ~1,000 common epigenomic hotspots. The atlas - publicly available at epiage.net - points to cytokine modulators as candidate systemic anti-aging drugs.

title: Sex-Dimorphic Immune Aging Demands Sex-Specific Geroprotective Protocols
description: Lifespan.io's lead story this week confirms that the immune system ages very differently in men and women - females trend toward inflammatory and autoimmune shifts while males accumulate pre-leukemic clones. The finding argues for sex-stratified inflammaging biomarkers and tailored senolytic and anti-inflammatory interventions.

title: A4M SpringFest 2026 Anchors 'Brainspan' as Longevity Medicine's New Endpoint
description: Takeaways from A4M Longevity SpringFest 2026 formalize biological-age testing (GrimAge, DunedinPACE, OmicAge) as routine clinical inputs and introduce 'brainspan' - the years of high-level cognitive function - as the field's next concrete endpoint. Simple tools like a 10-second balance test become early screens, shifting focus from late-stage care to early intervention.

title: NLRP3, Cellular Reprogramming and Mitochondria All Hit the Clinic This Week
description: Three distinct longevity mechanisms are generating positive human data in the same window: BioAge's BGE-102 (NLRP3 inhibitor) Phase 1 readout, Life Biosciences' Phase 1 partial epigenetic reprogramming now funded by its $80M Series D, and Mitrix Bio's mitochondrial transplantation moving into Right-to-Try clinics. Collectively, these programs move aging biology from bench to patient faster than at any prior point.

title: How the Body Really Ages: 7 Million Cells Mapped Across 21 Organs
description: Rockefeller University team publishes in Science the most detailed atlas yet of aging across 21 mouse tissues, showing coordinated systemic aging and identifying cytokines as candidate intervention targets.

title: The Longevity Trade Is Now a $120B Market - FDA Clears First Cell Rejuvenation Trial
description: Equity-Insider market commentary reports FDA clearance of the first cellular rejuvenation trial, an $8.49B 2025 private capital base, $65B in biotech M&A through Oct 2025, and GLP-1's new status as 'longevity therapeutics'.

title: Global Experts Highlight New Strategies to Target Human Aging
description: Aging-US publishes the 12th ARDD meeting report on actionable interventions in human aging (Dekan et al., 2026), consolidating translational priorities for the field.

title: The Immune System Ages Differently in Men and Women
description: Single-cell RNA study of ~1,000 people identifies sex-divergent immune aging patterns, with women showing sharper inflammatory shifts and men more pre-leukemic clones.

title: Rapamycin Might Blunt Exercise Response in Humans
description: New human data suggest chronic rapamycin can dampen exercise adaptations in older subjects, raising practical protocol questions for geroprotective dosing.

title: BioAge Reports Positive Phase 1 Data for BGE-102
description: BioAge's NLRP3-inhibitor program for metabolic diseases of aging shows positive Phase 1 tolerability and early activity signals, supporting later-stage development.

title: Top Trends from A4M Longevity SpringFest 2026
description: Key takeaways include biological-age testing entering routine clinical care, 'brainspan' as the new cognitive endpoint, and stress/patient-led care models shaping future-ready longevity practice.

title: Life Biosciences Secures $80M Series D Financing
description: David Sinclair co-founded Life Bio closes $80M to complete Phase 1 ER-100 trial in optic neuropathies and extend its partial epigenetic reprogramming platform runway.

title: How Inflammaging Is Linked to Epigenetic Aging
description: A Cell Genomics paper ties age-related systemic inflammation to epigenetic aging across four established clocks, connecting two core aging hallmarks.

title: Engineered Stem Cells Become Lifelong Protein Factories
description: Researchers engineer blood stem cells into durable in vivo protein producers - a platform with potential application to chronic age-related diseases.

title: Targeting an Appetite Hormone Receptor for Stronger Muscles
description: Suppressing the ghrelin receptor improves muscle function and fights sarcopenia in older mice, complementing GLP-1-era concerns about muscle loss.

title: Mitrix Moves Mitochondria into the Clinic
description: Mitrix Bio reports Phase 1 safety with transplanted mitochondria in two older participants and opens clinics in Newport Beach, Dallas, and Palm Beach under Right to Try.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment 2026: Set for a Breakout Year
description: DLT Chatbot estimates full-year 2026 longevity funding of $8-9B (55-60% above 2025's $5.72B), with Q1 at $3.74B across 49 deals.

title: Longevity Biotech Raises $8-9B in 2026, Signaling Industry Shift
description: Commentary highlights cellular reprogramming (Altos $3B), consumer longevity (Function, Neko, Viome >$700M), and marquee investors (Altman, Armstrong, Diamandis) driving the category.

title: Longevity Startup Funding 2025-2026: 14 Deals, $793M, Top-Heavy
description: 12-month pure-play longevity dataset: $792.9M across 14 disclosed rounds, 81% of capital concentrated in the top 5 deals; Series B dominates at 63% of dollars.

title: Longevity Market News - April 2026 Roundup
description: 86% of consumers prefer healthspan over lifespan, ARPA-H's PROSPR program aims to shorten aging-trial timelines, and Rubedo's senolytic Phase 1 marks one of the first clinical signals.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches 84-Person Rapamycin Aging Trial
description: NIA-funded randomized placebo-controlled rapamycin study in older adults evaluates dosing and short/long-term effects on healthy aging.

title: UT Health San Antonio 700-Adult Rapamycin/Semaglutide/Dapagliflozin Study
description: One of the largest geroscience trials to date tests three existing drugs for anti-aging signals in 700+ older adults at the Barshop Institute.

title: Brown/Rochester Win Up to $22M to Test Censavudine for Aging
description: ARPA-H-funded project will test whether the HIV drug Censavudine can quiet DNA-triggered chronic inflammation in 200+ adults aged 60-65 over 48 weeks.

title: Longeveron Phase 2b: Lomecel-B Reverses Age-Related Frailty
description: Cell Stem Cell publication reports dose-dependent 6MWT gains and 30.8% of patients moving to non-frail status after Longeveron's mesenchymal stem cell therapy.

title: Rubedo's RLS-1496 Shows Positive Preliminary Senolytic Signal
description: Rubedo's Phase 1 ascending-dose RCT in plaque psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and skin aging reported preliminary positive results - one of the first senolytic clinical signals.

title: Vitamin C Alleviates Aging in Cynomolgus Monkeys
description: A primate study positions vitamin C as a translatable geroprotective lever via ACSL4 inhibition and reduced ferroptosis.

title: David Sinclair Backs NAD+ Plus CD38 Inhibition Longevity Stack
description: Sinclair endorses a dual approach - boosting NAD+ precursors while inhibiting the NAD-consuming enzyme CD38 - as a next-gen geroprotective stack.

title: Updated Longevity Protocols for 2026 - Sinclair and Diamandis
description: Protocol notes from the Abundance Summit cover NMN 1g, resveratrol, nattokinase, spermidine, glycine, plant-based diet, pulsed fasting and 3-day fasts.

title: Bryan Johnson's Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update
description: 2026 refinements emphasize organ-specific metrics and frequent labs, reduce NMN/NR frequency, and add low-dose lithium and NDGA.

title: Peloton Named Movement Partner for The Longevity Show 2026
description: Peloton's new Longevity Show partnership deepens consumer-fitness integration across the longevity ecosystem.

title: Generare Bags $21.6M for Nature-Derived Drug Leads
description: Biotech Generare raises $21.6M to advance natural-product derived drug leads relevant to longevity pathways.

title: ITP: Astaxanthin, Meclizine, Pioglitazone and Others Fail to Extend Lifespan
description: The Interventions Testing Program reports that 11 candidate compounds including astaxanthin, meclizine, and pioglitazone did not extend lifespan in UM-HET3 mice; some reduced female lifespan.

title: PEPITEM Replacement Therapy Shows Potential for Early-Stage Inflammatory Arthritis
description: Synthetic PEPITEM prevented arthritis onset in mouse models and outperformed infliximab on swelling and joint damage, suggesting a new inflammaging-linked therapy route.
